title: Information Retrieval Foundations and Inverted Indexes · Program Brief: Rag Boolean Set Retrieval
summary: A team calls substring matching retrieval, cannot define a document or relevance judgment, and returns results in hash-map order when scores tie. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the boolean set retrieval increment in inspectable mini search engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Implement AND, OR, NOT, phrase, and field constraints using explicit set semantics and precedence.
Model corpus, document, field, query, information need, relevance, judgment, candidate, rank, and cutoff as distinct objects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-ir-foundations-index-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-ir-foundations-index-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_ir_foundations_index_4(document_count: int = 8, term_count: int = 5, posting_count: int = 3, query_terms: int = 2, deterministic_order: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if document_count <= 0 or term_count <= 0:
        return False, 0, "corpus-shape"
    if posting_count < 0 or posting_count > document_count:
        return False, posting_count, "posting-list"
    if query_terms <= 0 or query_terms > term_count:
        return False, query_terms, "query-model"
    if not deterministic_order:
        return False, posting_count, "unstable-ranking"
    matched = min(posting_count, query_terms + 1)
    assert 0 <= matched <= document_count
    return True, matched, "inverted-index"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Implement AND, OR, NOT, phrase, and field constraints using explicit set semantics and precedence.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Boolean Set Retrieval model · Theory Rag Boolean Set Retrieval, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Boolean Set Retrieval precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Boolean Set Retrieval model · Theory Rag Boolean Set Retrieval, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 8748 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to model corpus, query, terms, postings, Boolean matching, and deterministic ranked evidence; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement AND, OR, NOT, phrase, and field constraints using explicit set semantics and precedence.
